commitMessage: Revert "Reapply "Reapply "Reapply "Add R key to reset game from any state""""

topics.title: Remove reset keyboard shortcut and update player instructions
topics.trigger: A previously added feature allowing players to press the R key to reset the game from any state was reverted, removing that capability from the game.
topics.response:
- The R key handler was removed from `src/main.ts`, eliminating the ability to trigger a new game via keyboard at any point during play.
- The on-screen control hint in `index.html` was updated to remove the "Press R to Reset" instruction, keeping the displayed controls accurate.
- A call to hide the pause screen overlay when starting a new game was also removed from the `startNewGame` flow in `src/main.ts`.
topics.decisions:
- **Reverting rather than adjusting the feature**: The entire reset-via-keyboard feature was rolled back rather than patched or refined. This suggests the implementation introduced a problem significant enough to warrant full removal — likely a regression or unintended interaction — rather than a targeted fix, which would have been faster but riskier if the root cause was unclear.
- **Removing the pause-screen hide call alongside the key handler**: The cleanup of the pause overlay dismissal was bundled into this revert rather than kept as a standalone improvement. Keeping it would have left a partial remnant of the reverted feature's side effects in the codebase, which could cause confusion or subtle bugs later.

recap: The ability to reset the snake game by pressing the R key was removed. This keyboard shortcut had been added and re-added across several prior commits, but this commit reverts it entirely, leaving players without a mid-game reset option from the keyboard. The on-screen instructions visible to players were updated to match, no longer advertising a reset key that no longer works. A related change to how the pause screen was dismissed when starting a new game was also rolled back as part of the same revert.
